The text describes a new AI threat involving the creation of adversarial car stickers made of ultra-thin aluminum films. Unlike previous methods, these stickers modify the surface emissivity of a car, rather than its temperature. The goal is to deceive infrared autonomous driving systems by creating adversarial shadow patterns that are undetectable to target detectors such as Faster R-CNN.

The text discusses a research on AI threat involving an attack on object detection systems, specifically focusing on autonomous vehicles. The attack mechanism involves creating adversarial car textures optimized for Faster RCNN, a type of object detection system. The attack is successful at various viewing angles and distances, outperforming many previous methods which were effective only within limited angles and shorter distances.
